 {"id":"bd-fpvkd","title":"add mounted-path ext4 renameat2 flag-rejection coverage for NOREPLACE and EXCHANGE","description":"Survey finding: ffs-fuse::rename currently rejects any nonzero rename flags with EINVAL, but fuse_e2e only covers plain rename success paths. Scope: add mounted-path ext4 probes that invoke renameat2 with RENAME_NOREPLACE and RENAME_EXCHANGE (or another representative nonzero flag set) via libc/ctypes, assert exact EINVAL, and verify source/destination names and contents remain unchanged. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E freezes the public rename flag-rejection contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:45:29.512905710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:40:24.251586455Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:40:24.251586455Z","close_reason":"Added mounted-path ext4 renameat2 unsupported-flag EINVAL coverage for RENAME_NOREPLACE and RENAME_EXCHANGE; verified via focused rch test plus fmt/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-g1dum","title":"Freeze exact ThrottleInjector debug contract","description":"Replace substring-only assertions in crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs::throttle_debug_format with an exact golden contract for the custom ThrottleInjector debug output, then verify via rch targeted tests plus fmt/check/clippy.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T05:28:26.528327973Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T05:34:01.297133332Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T05:34:01.294662366Z","close_reason":"Froze exact ThrottleInjector debug output contract; verified via rch targeted test plus workspace fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-block","golden-artifacts","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-g1ue","title":"Harden ffs-dir test coverage with property-based and boundary tests","description":"## What
Expand ffs-dir from 49 tests to 70+ with property-based tests (proptest) and additional
boundary/corruption tests.

## Why
ffs-dir has the lowest test count (49) of any non-trivial crate. Directory operations are
safety-critical: a bug in add_entry/remove_entry can corrupt the entire directory structure.
The crate has proptest as a dev-dependency but no property-based tests yet.

## Current Coverage (49 tests)
- init_dir_block: 5 tests (basic, tail, sizes, inode numbers)
- add_entry: 10 tests (split, reuse, enospc, reject, max name, types)
- remove_entry: 7 tests (coalesce, first entry, nonexistent, overlap)
- htree: 8 tests (insert, find, remove, edge cases)
- compute_dx_hash: 3 tests (deterministic, seeds, names)
- Multi-operation: 3 tests (fill, cycle, alternating)
- Corruption detection: 3 tests (rec_len zero, misaligned, exceeds block)

## Gaps to Fill
1. Property-based tests (proptest):
   - proptest_add_remove_roundtrip: random names (1..255 bytes), random inodes,
     add then remove → block parseable, entry gone
   - proptest_add_never_exceeds_block: random sequence of adds → never writes past limit
   - proptest_init_parse_roundtrip: random self_ino, parent_ino, reserved_tail →
     init_dir_block → parse_dir_block → exactly . and .. with correct inodes
   - proptest_htree_insert_always_sorted: random hash sequence → entries always sorted

2. Boundary tests:
   - add_entry with exactly 0 bytes of slack (entry fits perfectly, no split needed)
   - remove_entry of last entry in block (rec_len extends to block end)
   - init_dir_block with reserved_tail = block_size - 24 (minimum viable)
   - add_entry with name at every alignment boundary (1,2,3,4,5,8,12,16 bytes)

3. Corruption resilience:
   - add_entry with rec_len pointing past block boundary
   - remove_entry with circular rec_len chain (entry points back to itself)
   - add_entry to block of all zeros (no valid first entry)

4. Stress tests:
   - Fill 4096-byte block completely, verify parse_dir_block recovers all entries
   - Add 100 entries, remove 50, add 50 more, verify final state

## Implementation Plan
Add to the existing #[cfg(test)] module in crates/ffs-dir/src/lib.rs.
proptest is already a dev-dependency of ffs-dir.

## Test Plan
- cargo test -p ffs-dir --lib → all pass, count >= 70
- proptest cases: 128 per property test
- No flakes in 3 consecutive runs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 4+ property-based tests added
- [ ] 5+ boundary tests added
- [ ] 3+ corruption resilience tests added
- [ ] 2+ stress tests added
- [ ] Total test count >= 70
- [ ] Zero test flakes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:18:00.522669212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:42:36.913247920Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T21:42:36.912968691Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-g30","title":"Track: CLI + TUI (ffs-cli + ffs-tui)","description":"Provide operator tooling: CLI subcommands (inspect/info/fsck/mount), and a frankentui-based dashboard to observe cache/MVCC/repair stats.\n\nAcceptance: CLI is the primary UX for harness + debugging; TUI can attach to a running mount to show internal metrics. Both must be deterministic/testable and use ffs-core as the integration surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:24.040606496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:44:14.103200953Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:44:14.103175686Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: clap CLI, mount, fsck/scrub, TUI dashboard","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-23n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:55.362716892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-23r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:55.620739199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-2fs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:55.448460236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-3sk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:12.268311230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-3sp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:12.183821788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-3vn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:55.275857697Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-g30","depends_on_id":"bd-m35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:55.535816523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-g30","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Operator UX track.

CLI is the debugging and automation surface:
- stable JSON output options
- mount/fsck workflows
- parity/stats commands

TUI is phased and depends on metrics surfaces from cache/MVCC/repair.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}

@@ -155,6 +155,9 @@ overwrites, with no root-entry, source-child, or file-byte drift.
 Mounted-path btrfs rename coverage now also freezes same-name source/destination
 semantics as a visible no-op, with no directory-entry drift and no inode/data
 drift.
+Mounted-path btrfs rename refusal coverage now also freezes exact `EISDIR` for
+file-over-directory overwrites and exact `ENOTDIR` for directory-over-file
+overwrites, with no root-entry, source-child, or file-byte drift.
 Mounted-path btrfs cross-parent directory rename coverage now also freezes
 parent `st_nlink` accounting: moving a child directory decrements the source
 parent, increments the destination parent, and preserves the moved inode.
